Title: Arabidopsis C-terminal binding protein ANGUSTIFOLIA modulates transcriptional co-regulation of MYB46 and WRKY33

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: salicylic acid; jasmonic acid/ethylene-associated defense output
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defense against hemibiotrophic pathogens via SA pathway and against necrotrophic pathogens via JA/ET pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: transcriptional coregulator / transcriptional repressor-like CtBP homolog
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: C-terminal binding protein (CtBP) homolog; ANGUSTIFOLIA family
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pseudomonas syringae infection; Botrytis cinerea infection
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses MYB46 transcription directly; indirectly relieves TDP1-mediated repression of WRKY33
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: direct transcriptional repression of MYB46 promoter binding; sequestration/counteraction of TDP1 on WRKY33 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: coordination of SA biosynthesis-associated transcription and JA/ET defense signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: coordinates antagonism between SA and JA/ET pathways by co-regulating MYB46 and WRKY33
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae DC3000 infection; Botrytis cinerea infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: AN transcription and nuclear accumulation are altered upon pathogen infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: pathogen-responsive in leaves; nuclear accumulation reduced early after P. syringae infection
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: reduces resistance to P. syringae and enhances resistance to B. cinerea; represses SA-associated gene expression and promotes JA/ET-associated gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutant analysis; complementation/overexpression; pathogen assays; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization; cell fractionation; yeast two-hybrid; transactivation assay; ChIP-qPCR; microChIP-qPCR; EMSA; SA measurement
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: functions with TDP1 in an AN-TDP1 regulatory complex
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (previous analysis cited in paper; accession NA)
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: antagonistic defense trade-off: enhanced biotroph resistance is associated with reduced necrotroph resistance, and vice versa
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: modulating AN could tune SA-JA/ET defense balance for pathogen resistance engineering, but requires caution because of defense trade-offs and developmental roles
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to hormone-defense balance, AN is not a metabolic enzyme; it acts upstream of PAL genes via MYB46 and WRKY33. Paper shows pathogen-induced SA accumulation changes consistent with AN activity.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AN antagonistically regulates resistance to P. syringae and B. cinerea. 2. AN is a nuclear/cytoplasmic CtBP homolog with transcriptional repressor activity. 3. AN directly binds and represses MYB46 and indirectly promotes WRKY33 expression by counteracting TDP1, thereby shifting SA and JA/ET pathway outputs.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: salicylic acid; jasmonic acid/ethylene-associated defense output
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: defense pathway coordination against hemibiotrophic and necrotrophic pathogens
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: transcription-related coregulator with DNA repair enzyme function
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Tyrosyl-DNA phosphodiesterase 1; FHA domain plus TDP domain
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; also detected in cytosolic fraction
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses WRKY33 transcription; enhances AN-mediated repression of MYB46 by promoting AN nuclear accumulation and promoter association
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression; protein interaction-mediated enhancement of AN promoter occupancy
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: coordination of SA-related PAL branch repression and JA/ET defense regulator expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: serves as the AN-interacting factor required for full repression of MYB46 and as a direct repressor of WRKY33, thereby affecting SA/JA-ET antagonism
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae DC3000 infection; Botrytis cinerea infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: negatively co-expressed with WRKY33 across tissues and pathogen infections
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enhances AN-mediated repression of MYB46 and represses WRKY33; tdp1-2 mutant shows increased resistance to both P. syringae and B. cinerea
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast two-hybrid; co-localization; cell fractionation; transactivation assay; qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; microChIP-qPCR; mutant pathogen assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: co-expression analysis using AtGenExpress Visualization Tool; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss of TDP1 increases resistance phenotypes but TDP1 also has DNA repair roles, implying possible genome maintenance trade-offs
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: targeting TDP1 may reprogram defense hormone balance, but its DNA repair function may constrain deployment
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TDP1 is not a canonical nutrient metabolism regulator, but is a core mechanistic regulator in this paper because it directly modulates MYB46/WRKY33 transcriptional control linked to SA and JA/ET outputs.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TDP1 physically interacts with AN via its FHA-containing region and increases AN nuclear accumulation. 2. TDP1 is required for strong AN association with and repression of MYB46. 3. TDP1 directly represses WRKY33, while AN relieves this repression, together coordinating SA and JA/ET pathway gene expression.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter tested in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: phenylpropanoid branch contributing to SA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: acts as transcriptional activator of PAL genes required for SA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated in an-t1 mutant background relative to wild type
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes PAL expression and supports SA biosynthesis-associated defense; is repressed by AN
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: microChIP-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA for AN binding to promoter; transactivation assay with MYB46 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: could be manipulated to increase PAL-driven SA-associated defense, but may interact with broader NAC-MYB network causing pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this paper MYB46 is treated as a direct AN target and upstream activator of PAL genes; it is a mechanistic node rather than the primary manipulated transgene.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB46 promoter is directly bound by AN. 2. AN-TDP1 represses MYB46 transcription. 3. MYB46 positively regulates PAL genes linked to SA biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter tested in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: TDP1 repression; AN-TDP1 interaction; pathogen defense context
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: JA/ET signaling output for necrotroph defense
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: master regulator of JA/ET signaling pathway in pathogen defense
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: negatively co-expressed with TDP1 across tissues and pathogen infections; downregulated in an-t1 mutant
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: supports JA/ET defense gene expression; TDP1 represses WRKY33 and AN alleviates this repression
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: co-expression analysis using AtGenExpress Visualization Tool; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: candidate for enhancing necrotroph resistance through JA/ET pathway activation, with possible trade-offs against SA-mediated defense
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: WRKY33 was not directly bound by AN in the assays; regulation occurs through TDP1 repression relieved by AN interaction.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY33 is directly repressed by TDP1 at the promoter level. 2. AN relieves TDP1-imposed repression of WRKY33. 3. WRKY33 activates JA/ET pathway genes important for B. cinerea resistance.
